title: Run scheduler cycles asynchronously in TUI
description:
The TUI's run_scheduler_cycle() blocks the Textual event loop while beads execute (5-10 minutes per bead). The TUI freezes completely. Fix this in two parts:

PART 1 - Async scheduler: Run scheduler cycles in a Textual worker thread (self.run_worker or asyncio.to_thread) so the event loop stays responsive. Prevent launching a second cycle while one is running. Both s (single pass) and S (continuous) should use the async path. Support --max-workers > 1 for parallel bead execution within a cycle.

PART 2 - Refactor status panel into a live scheduler log: The current status panel crams 7 lines of mixed concerns (status, mode, activity, last action, last result, footer) into a fixed-height area. Refactor it into two sections: (a) A compact status bar (1-2 lines) showing mode, active panel, and last action. (b) A scrollable scheduler log area below it that streams scheduler events in real-time as they happen — bead started, worktree ready, bead completed/blocked/failed, deferred. For multiple parallel beads, each event should be prefixed with the bead ID so interleaved output is readable. Create a TUI-specific SchedulerReporter implementation that posts events to the Textual app (e.g. via call_from_thread and a message queue) instead of writing to a StringIO stream. The log should auto-scroll to the latest entry but allow the user to scroll back.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler cycles run in a background thread, not blocking the Textual event loop
The TUI remains responsive (redraws, key input, mouse) while a scheduler cycle is running
A status indicator shows when a scheduler cycle is in progress
A second scheduler cycle cannot be launched while one is already running
Both s (single pass) and S (continuous) use the async path
The bead list refreshes when the scheduler cycle completes

handoff_summary.completed: PART 1: Async scheduler - worker thread execution, double-launch guard, both s/S async, max_workers support. PART 2: Status panel refactored into compact status bar + scrollable RichLog scheduler log with TuiSchedulerReporter streaming live events from worker thread.
handoff_summary.risks: The `_make_services` wrapper creates a new Scheduler instance per cycle (same as before). RichLog widget may accumulate unbounded entries over very long sessions - no cap implemented.
handoff_summary.next_action: Test and review the async scheduler and live log implementation.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: tests/test_tui.py updated test assertions for new status panel format and scheduler cycle API - may conflict with other beads modifying TUI tests.

conflict_risks: tests/test_tui.py updated test assertions for new status panel format and scheduler cycle API - may conflict with other beads modifying TUI tests.

Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B0130-test

Implemented async scheduler execution and live scheduler log in the TUI. Key changes: (1) Scheduler cycles now run in a Textual worker thread via `run_worker()`, keeping the event loop responsive. (2) A `scheduler_running` guard prevents double-launching. (3) Both `s` (single) and `S` (continuous) use the async worker path. (4) Created `TuiSchedulerReporter` that posts real-time scheduler events to a `RichLog` widget via `call_from_thread`. (5) Refactored the status panel into a compact 2-line status bar (`#status-bar`) plus a scrollable scheduler log (`#scheduler-log` RichLog widget with auto-scroll). (6) Added `max_workers` parameter through the full stack: CLI `--max-workers` arg, `command_tui`, `run_tui`, `build_tui_app`, `TuiRuntimeState`, and `scheduler.run_once()`. All 68 existing TUI tests pass.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
